Let us now move on to rejection candles and see how to use these in our daily trading.

How to Trade Rejection Candles

Rejection Candle Can End an Ongoing Trend

As we learnt earlier, candles with ex
rule_text: When a rejection candle (candle with extremely long tail) forms and gets a followthrough in the opposite direction, it signals a trend reversal and the beginning of a new trend in that opposite direction.

The second kind is when the opening price ticks fall outside the previous day's range, namely when the price either breaks out above the previous day's range or breaks down below it.

Trading the Open
rule_text: When opening ticks fall within the previous day's range, buy at the lower boundary with upper boundary as target, or sell at the upper boundary with lower boundary as target, continuing until either boundary is broken.

OR sell at the upper boundary of the previous day's range
